Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me explore the template-related files more to understand what's already there, and then provide a comprehensive answer about implementing the canvas-based invoice editor with:
1. Background PNG upload
2. Drag and drop field placement
3. Google Fonts integration
4. Pantone color picker

Now I have a very complete picture of what exists. Let me lay out exactly what this feature involves, how it fits into the codebase, and what needs to be built.

---

## What You're Describing — The "Canvas Mode" Invoice Editor

This is essentially a new mode alongside your 3 existing templates (Classic, Modern, Professional). Think of it as **"Canvas"** — you upload your own designed background (a PNG), and then place the invoice data fields anywhere on top of it by dragging them.

Here's the full picture of what I'd build:

---

### How It Works (User Flow)

```
Templates page
  └── New "Canvas" button
        └── Canvas Editor opens
              ├── Upload background PNG (your design / letterhead)
              ├── Each invoice field appears as a draggable chip
              │     (Invoice #, Date, Client Name, Amount, Table, etc.)
              ├── Drag fields to exact position on the canvas
              ├── Per-field controls: font, font size, color
              ├── Google Fonts picker in the sidebar
              └── Pantone color picker for colors
```

When you generate a PDF, the PNG background + positioned field text overlay combine — exactly like printing on pre-printed letterhead.

---

### What Needs to Be Built

#### 1. Extend `TemplateConfig` in `lib/template-defaults.ts`

Add a new `"canvas"` templateId and new canvas-specific properties:

```typescript
// New fields to add to TemplateConfig
canvasBackground?: string   // URL of uploaded PNG in Supabase Storage
fieldLayout?: {
  [fieldKey: string]: {
    x: number         // % from left  
    y: number         // % from top
    fontSize?: number
    color?: string
    fontFamily?: string
    show: boolean
    width?: number    // % width of the chip
  }
}
```

All positions are stored as **percentages** (not pixels) so the layout scales correctly between screen preview and print/PDF.

#### 2. New `CanvasTemplateEditor` component

A full-screen editor with:
- **Left sidebar** — field list (toggle show/hide), per-field font/size/color
- **Center canvas** — A4 paper with PNG background, draggable field chips
- **Drag logic** — Pure React `onMouseDown` / `onMouseMove` / `onMouseUp` — no extra library needed

#### 3. PNG Upload to Supabase Storage

- A simple file input → upload to a `template-backgrounds` bucket in Supabase Storage
- Store the public URL in `config.canvasBackground`
- New API route: `POST /api/template-backgrounds`

#### 4. `CanvasTemplatePreview` component

Same as `TemplatePreview` but renders absolutely-positioned fields over the background PNG. This is what `[id]/pdf/page.tsx` uses at print time.

#### 5. Google Fonts Integration

Replace the 6 static system fonts in `FONT_OPTIONS` with a **Google Fonts browser** in the style panel:
- Embed a curated list of ~150 popular Google Fonts (no API key needed)
- Dynamically inject `<link>` tags: `https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=...`
- Font previews in the dropdown

```typescript
// Example — font is loaded on-demand
function loadGoogleFont(family: string) {
  const link = document.createElement("link")
  link.href = `https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=${family.replace(/ /g, "+")}&display=swap`
  link.rel = "stylesheet"
  document.head.appendChild(link)
}
```

#### 6. Pantone Color Picker

`pantonecolors.net` doesn't have a public API — but I can embed a curated dataset of ~500 popular Pantone colors (names, HEX, RGB, CMYK) directly in the codebase. The UI would look like this:

```
[ Search: "warm red" ]  [ Category: Fashion ▾ ]
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ● PANTONE 485 C    #DA291C    RGB(218,41,28) │
│ ● PANTONE 032 C    #EF3340    RGB(239,51,64) │
│ ● PANTONE Warm Red #F9423A    RGB(249,66,58) │
│ ...                                          │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Clicking a color copies it to the field's color picker. Since you'll be working within a design context, I'd organize by **Pantone Fashion + Home + Graphics** categories.

---

### Where Things Plug Into Existing Code

| Existing file | What changes |
|---|---|
| `lib/template-defaults.ts` | Add `"canvas"` to `templateId`, add `canvasBackground`, `fieldLayout` to `TemplateConfig` |
| `components/template-editor.tsx` | Add a 3rd tab "Canvas" that loads `CanvasTemplateEditor` |
| `components/template-style-panel.tsx` | Replace font options with Google Fonts picker; add Pantone picker |
| `app/dashboard/invoices/templates/page.tsx` | Add "Canvas Template" card option |
| `components/template-preview.tsx` | Add canvas rendering path |

**New files needed:**
- `components/canvas-template-editor.tsx` — the main drag canvas
- `components/canvas-template-preview.tsx` — print-safe render
- `components/google-fonts-picker.tsx` — font browser
- `components/pantone-picker.tsx` — color browser
- `lib/google-fonts-list.ts` — 150 popular fonts list
- `lib/pantone-colors.ts` — 500 Pantone color dataset
- `app/api/template-backgrounds/route.ts` — PNG upload handler

---

### A Note on the Pantone Website

The URL you shared (pantonecolors.net) doesn't expose an API, so I cannot fetch live data from it. What I'll do instead is embed a comprehensive offline dataset of Pantone colors with exact HEX/RGB/CMYK values — the same data that Pantone publishes. This works offline, is instant, and you can search/filter by name.

---

### Rough Build Order

1. Extend `TemplateConfig` types + DB schema if needed
2. PNG upload API + Supabase Storage bucket
3. `CanvasTemplateEditor` with drag-drop
4. `CanvasTemplatePreview` for PDF/print
5. Google Fonts picker
6. Pantone color picker
7. Wire into existing Templates page

I'd add a "Canvas" mode as a fourth tab in the template editor where users can upload a PNG background to Supabase Storage and drag fields around to position them freely, storing the x/y coordinates in the TemplateConfig. For Pantone colors, I can either scrape from pantonecolors.net or use a curated dataset of Pantone values in HEX/RGB/CMYK format. For the implementation, I'm proposing a phased approach starting with a new canvas mode in the template system. I'd extend the TemplateConfig to include the background image URL and a field layout object that tracks each field's position, size, color, font, and visibility state. Then I'd build dedicated editor and preview components specifically for this canvas mode, plus set up a Supabase Storage bucket for the background images.

Next I'm planning to add Google Fonts integration into the style panel for typography options, and include a Pantone color picker with a built-in dataset for color selection.

Files Overview ``` New files (6): lib/google-fonts-data.ts top-150 fonts from existing google-fonts.csv (by Popularity Rank) lib/pantone-colors-data.ts ~320 curated Pantone colors with name/hex/rgb/cmyk components/canvas-template-editor.tsx main drag canvas (A4 + chips) components/canvas-template-preview.tsx print-safe render (for pdf/page) components/google-fonts-picker.tsx searchable font browser components/pantone-color-picker.tsx searchable color browser app/api/template-backgrounds/route.ts PNG upload → Supabase Storage Modified files (4): lib/template-defaults.ts add "canvas" to TemplateConfig union components/template-editor.tsx add canvas mode entry components/templates-page-client.tsx add "Canvas" card app/dashboard/invoices/[id]/pdf/page.tsx handle canvas render path ``` ## Canvas Drag Logic Pure React pointer events — no new library. A4 div is `position: relative`. Each field chip is `position: absolute` with `left: x%`, `top: y%`. On `onPointerDown` we store which chip is dragging; `onPointerMove` updates `x/y` in local state; `onPointerUp` commits. Positions are percentages so they scale to any screen size and to print. ## Google Fonts - `lib/google-fonts-data.ts` — parse existing [`Design_skill_reference/data/google-fonts.csv`](Design_skill_reference/data/google-fonts.csv), filter to top 150 by `Popularity Rank`, keep `Family`, `Category`, `Styles` - Font is loaded on demand: inject `` into `` when selected - `components/google-fonts-picker.tsx` — searchable list grouped by category (Sans Serif / Serif / Display / Handwriting / Monospace), preview text "Aa" ## Pantone Colors pantonecolors.net has no public API. Clicking a swatch sets the active field's color. ## PNG Upload Flow ``` User picks file → POST /api/template-backgrounds (multipart) → supabase.storage.from("template-backgrounds").upload(path, file) → returns publicUrl → stored in config.canvasBackground ``` - Supabase Storage bucket `template-backgrounds` needs to be created (public read, auth required for write) - Max file size: 5MB, accepted types: image/png, image/jpeg ## Print / PDF Compatibility `CanvasTemplatePreview` renders: ```

// Returns CSS font-family string for use in styles
export function getFontFamily(family: string): string {
  const cat = GOOGLE_FONTS.find(f => f.family === family)?.category ?? "Sans Serif"
  const fallback = cat === "Serif" ? "serif"
    : cat === "Monospace" ? "monospace"
    : cat === "Handwriting" ? "cursive"
    : "sans-serif"
  return `'${family}', ${fallback}`
}

// Injects a Google Fonts <link> tag into <head> if not already loaded
export function loadGoogleFont(family: string): void {
  if (typeof document === "undefined") return
  const id = `gf-${family.replace(/\s+/g, "-")}`
  if (document.getElementById(id)) return
  const link = document.createElement("link")
  link.id   = id
  link.rel  = "stylesheet"
  link.href = `https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=${encodeURIComponent(family)}:wght@400;500;600;700&display=swap`
  document.head.appendChild(link)
}

// Unique categories for filtering
export const FONT_CATEGORIES = ["All", "Sans Serif", "Serif", "Display", "Handwriting", "Monospace"] as const
